ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Petition To Approve Report Of Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Aug-21-2018 Continued To Sep-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christine Del Sherpa appeared by telephone for Administrator.

The Court stated that it is still looking for a petition for final distribution, that it understands Ms. Del Sherpa is having some challenges and questions if it is time to hand this off. Ms. Del Sherpa stated that she thinks she can get it done, spoke of her situation/circumstances, and stated that she has been working on the second status report and that it is 90% done. Ms. Del Sherpa also stated that there is an issue with the Internal Revenue Service ("IRS") that has been holding them up forever, that it's not really a tax issue, that the Decedent paid taxes, but never signed a tax return which incurred many penalties, that they started receiving letters from the IRS in January 2018 and demands for payment three months later, and that they are thinking of doing a reserve for the tax payment. The Court informed Ms. Del Sherpa that she needs to file a petition for approval of a report on status of administration or a petition for final distribution. Status on close of estate is continued to September 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Chu. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jun-28-2017 Continued To Aug-09-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Claimant Adam Steinharts Petition To Allow Late Filing Of Claim Against Estate Probate Code Section 9103
Off Calendar Nov-16-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christine Del Sherpa for petitioner Robert Felter; Tracy Shows (by CourtCall) for Adam Steinhart.

Ms Shows argued that the late claim should be allowed on the grounds of equity. She stated that notice to the creditors was delayed for 60 days, and that the claimant had moved from his prior residence two days before the notice was mailed to his former address. She said that he did not actually receive the notice until June 24th, and that he thereafter acted promptly to obtain counsel and submit the claim. She contended that Probate Code section 9154(b) permits application of principles of equity to probate proceedings, and that case law permits the waiving of statutory time limits under equity. Ms. Del Sherpa responded that Mr. Steinhart was aware of his claim as of March, and that he had failed to notify Mr. Felter of his change of address or to make appropriate arrangements for mail sent to his former address to be forwarded. She argued that neither Probate Code section 9103 nor section 9154 was applicable or grounds for allowing the late claim. Mr. Felter stated that the parties wouldn't be in court today if Mr. Steinhart had just communicated with him. Ms. Shows stated that Mr. Steinhart always acted in good faith, the estate administrator was aware of the claim, and that there would be no prejudice if the late claim is allowed. The matter was taken under submission. Ms. Del Sherpa agreed to provide the Court with a proposed order denying the petition. Ms. Shows will submit an alternate proposed order which includes probate code section 9154 as alternative grounds for relief.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not reported)
